paper.title: IMMACULATE: A Practical LLM Auditing Framework via Verifiable Computation
paper.abstract: Commercial large language models are typically deployed as black-box API services, requiring users to trust providers to execute inference correctly and report token usage honestly. We present IMMACULATE, a practical auditing framework that detects economically motivated deviations-such as model substitution, quantization abuse, and token overbilling-without trusted hardware or access to model internals. IMMACULATE selectively audits a small fraction of requests using verifiable computation, ach

analysis.engineering_takeaway: IMMACULATE's selective auditing approach can be applied to other AI models to detect economically motivated deviations without trusted hardware or access to model internals.
technical_details: NVIDIA's Tensor Cores for hardware acceleration
analysis.deployment_feasibility: Yes, IMMACULATE can be deployed on both mobile and laptop platforms due to its low memory footprint and efficient use of hardware acceleration.

review_questions: Is this genuinely a breakthrough? Or incremental improvement?
